mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals impending recession risk, triggering negative earnings revisions and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and industrial production indicates robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint high values of HOUST and INDPRO do not predict positive 60-day returns above baseline, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a rolling regression over 24 months.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds historical median while the federal funds rate remains above 3.5 percent, real rates rise, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The negative return pattern disappears when CPIAUCSL is below its 2005 median, or forward returns show no significant difference between high-inflation/high-rate and low-inflation/low-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volume reflects immediate consumer spending strength, which translates into near-term earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS momentum over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal generates a negative Rank IC consistently across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below 105 signals supply-side contraction and capacity utilization stress, leading to negative earnings revisions and depressed S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO breaches the 105 threshold, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing over 18 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening, which historically precedes wage-driven consumption growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The positive return signal vanishes when PAYEMS growth is positive but UNRATE fails to decline, or the joint signal produces a negative information coefficient over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth exceeds 2.5 percent annualized and housing starts remain above 1.8 million units, the economy exhibits synchronized demand strength that drives sustained equity outperformance over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The positive return premium disappears when GDP growth slows below 2.0 percent despite high housing starts, or the trigger condition yields a win rate below 50 percent in walk-forward validation.
